name: cafefams-backend
description: Café Fams AI Chatbot Backend — v1.5.9: TASK 7 — platform-owner restaurant onboarding API, replacing hand-written SQL for adding a new restaurant. New env var PLATFORM_OWNER_KEY (the founder's own master key, separate from any restaurant's admin_password) + requirePlatformOwner middleware (mirrors requireAdmin, checks x-platform-key). POST /api/platform/restaurants inserts a restaurants row + all its menu_items rows in a single DB transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT via a pg pool client, ROLLBACK on any failure so a partial menu can never be left behind), validates id format/uniqueness/required fields before writing anything, invalidates the resolver cache on success, and never logs/echoes adminPassword/razorpayKeySecret/razorpayWebhookSecret/telegramBotToken. GET /api/platform/restaurants lists all onboarded restaurants with menu item counts. Both routes are rate-limited (platformOwnerLimiter, matches adminLoginLimiter's strictness) since this one key can create restaurants platform-wide. Schema: restaurants.created_at + the restaurants_id_format CHECK constraint were added directly via migration (see migration.sql) — additive only, verified 'cafefams' unaffected. Zero changes to any existing route, the resolver, its cafefams-only fallback, rate limiting, or Café Fams's live behavior. v1.5.8: TASK 6 — index.html itself is now multi-tenant. New public GET /api/branding (restaurantId query param, default 'cafefams') returns { name, assistantName, locationText, brandColor, logoEmoji } for a restaurant, resolved via the existing getRestaurant(); it gained two new columns (brand_color, logo_emoji, added by migration.sql) with the same per-field env/hardcoded fallback pattern as the other Plan B fields, defaulting to Café Fams's own current --brown2 (#6B3A25) and ☕ so its live site is unaffected. index.html now reads ?restaurant=<id> from the URL (default 'cafefams'), fetches /api/branding once at startup, and — only on success — re-themes its title, header/logo text, --brown2 CSS var, bot avatar emoji, and assistant name; a failed fetch silently keeps every Café Fams hardcoded default exactly as before. restaurantId is also now threaded into every customer-facing fetch call index.html makes that the backend already accepts it on (chat ×2, note, feedback, call-waiter, cancel, payment/create, orders/by-phone, assist-request); payment/verify and public/ratings were deliberately left unchanged since the backend doesn't read restaurantId from either (verify derives it from the order's own DB row for signature security). No behavior change for the live 'cafefams' restaurant when loaded with no ?restaurant= param. v1.5.7: Plan B Step 5 — per-restaurant admin login. requireAdmin and POST /api/admin/login now check the resolved restaurant's own admin_password (via getRestaurant(), restaurantId in body/query, default 'cafefams') instead of the single global ADMIN_PASSWORD constant; getRestaurant() gained the same per-field env-var fallback for admin_password that razorpay_*/telegram_* fields already had (was missing — cafefams's DB column is still NULL, so this was required to avoid locking out the live admin panel); POST /api/admin/radius-toggle's inline x-admin-key check (the one route not going through requireAdmin) updated to match. A restaurant with no admin_password configured gets an explicit 503 admin_login_not_configured instead of silently falling back to Café Fams's password. No behavior change for the live 'cafefams' admin login/dashboard — admin.html is unmodified and untouched by this change. v1.5.6: Plan B Step 4 — explicit restaurant_id scoping. Every INSERT into orders/feedback/assist_requests/activity_log now explicitly writes restaurant_id (previously relied on the column DEFAULT); every admin read route (orders/feedback/activity/assist-requests/stats) and the by-phone/cancel/discount lookups now filter WHERE restaurant_id=$N, with the in-memory fallback stores (orders{}/feedbackList[]/activityLog[]/assistRequests[]) also carrying restaurant_id so filtering stays consistent whether data comes from Supabase or memory; call-waiter/feedback/assist-request/note/cancel now accept the same optional restaurantId body param (default 'cafefams') as the other customer-facing routes; the legacy app_settings fallback keys (soldOutItems/geoRadiusEnabled) are now prefixed per-restaurant. No behavior change for the live 'cafefams' restaurant — all defaults resolve identically to before. v1.5.5: sold-out/geo-radius toggles now write directly to menu_items.sold_out / restaurants.geo_radius_enabled in Supabase (in addition to the existing in-memory Set/variable + app_settings, kept as a belt-and-suspenders fallback), with resolver cache invalidation on every toggle so the change is live within the same request instead of up to 15s later; removed the temporary in-memory overlay in getRestaurant()/getMenu() now that the DB is genuinely current; GET /api/admin/menu now sources from the same resolver customers' menus use, with its exact response shape preserved. v1.5.4: Plan B Step 2 — per-restaurant Telegram notification infrastructure. getRestaurant() now resolves telegram_bot_token/telegram_chat_id from the restaurants table (with a per-field env-var fallback for 'cafefams' only, same pattern as Razorpay), and a new sendTelegramForRestaurant() sender is wired into the 'New Order!' notification in POST /api/chat so a future second restaurant's order alerts can land on THEIR Telegram instead of the founder's; the other ~8 notification call sites are unchanged pending the separate data-isolation task; requires running migration.sql. v1.5.3: Plan B Step 1 — per-restaurant Razorpay payment credentials. /api/payment/create, /verify, and /webhook now resolve key_id/key_secret/webhook_secret from the restaurants table (with a per-field env-var fallback for 'cafefams' only), so a future second restaurant's payments can never be misdirected into Café Fams's own Razorpay account; requires running migration.sql. v1.5.2: adds a system-prompt rule preventing the AI from re-confirming (and duplicating) an order when a customer just wants to view their existing bill — paired with a frontend fix that stops the 'My Bill' button from ever reaching the AI at all. v1.5.1: IPv6/pooler connection diagnostics. v1.5.0: fixed silent data-loss bug (RLS policies blocked all Supabase writes), boot-time DB write self-test + Telegram alerting. v1.4.0: multi-tenant resolver, payment/price integrity, rate limiting, prompt-injection guardrails, memory/SSE fixes
